answer:
In horizontal microarchitecture, microinstructions are typically wide and unencoded, with many control bits directly controlling hardware resources, so statement II is true. Horizontal microinstructions usually do not have a single opcode and multiple operand specifiers; that description fits vertical microarchitecture, making statement III false. Statement I is also false because horizontal architectures can often accomplish tasks in fewer time steps due to greater parallelism.

\boxed{F}
